claims.summary: Cadence clarity and hybrid coherence are important gradients rather than binary connection labels.
claims.disposition: Verified current schemas preserve numeric timing, connection, coherence, delta, collision, and basis evidence.

claims.summary: Packet jitter may correlate with Astrid's reported restlessness.
claims.disposition: Routed to a bounded timestamp-to-felt-report association; no causal claim will be made from temporal alignment alone.

claims.summary: High hybrid coherence with high distinguishability loss may expose identity flattening.
claims.disposition: Routed to a copied telemetry comparison of independent coherence and distinguishability measures.

claims.summary: A float granularity bias should blend typed and legacy representations.
claims.disposition: Preserved as Tier 5 representation and protocol-semantics authority; no live precedence change is authorized.
